metadata.description: Name prefix for all resources (e.g., "myagentapp"). Resources will be named like {appName}-{resource}-{environment}. Auto-generated from the resource group ID if not provided.
Azure AI Foundry project endpoint URL (Required). Find in AI Foundry portal → Project → Settings → Endpoint. Format: https://<resource>.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/<project-name>
Azure AI Foundry connection name for OpenAI
Model deployment name in AI Foundry (e.g., gpt-4o, gpt-5.2)
Deploy new Azure OpenAI resource (true) or use existing (false). Leave false to use Foundry models (recommended).
Azure OpenAI account name (used when deployAzureOpenAI=true)
Azure OpenAI model name to deploy (e.g., gpt-4o, gpt-4, gpt-35-turbo)
Azure OpenAI model deployment capacity (TPM in thousands, 1-1000)
Azure OpenAI service endpoint URL (optional - only needed when deployAzureOpenAI=false and using separate Azure OpenAI)
Azure OpenAI deployment/model name (e.g., gpt-4o, gpt-35-turbo)
Azure OpenAI API Key (optional - only needed when using separate Azure OpenAI)
Azure AI Foundry Orchestrator Agent ID (Required)
Azure AI Foundry Sales Agent ID (Required)
Azure AI Foundry Realtime Agent ID (Required)
Azure AI Foundry Analytics Agent ID (Required)
Azure AI Foundry Financial Agent ID (Required)
Azure AI Foundry Support Agent ID (Required)
Azure AI Foundry Operations Coordinator Agent ID (Required)
Azure AI Foundry Customer Success Agent ID (Required)
Azure AI Foundry Operations Excellence Agent ID (Required)
Power BI workspace ID (often same as Fabric workspace)
Power BI report ID to embed
Power BI service principal client ID
Power BI service principal tenant ID
Power BI service principal client secret
SQL Server name (must be globally unique). Auto-generated from appName if not provided.
SQL Database SKU (Basic, S0, S1, P1, etc.)
Use Azure AD authentication for SQL (recommended)
SQL admin username (required if not using Azure AD only)
SQL admin password (required if not using Azure AD only)
Azure AD admin login name (e.g., admin@domain.com)
Enable JWT authentication for the application
JWT secret key (leave empty to auto-generate)
App Service Plan SKU (B1, B2, S1, P1v2, P2v2, etc.)
Enable Key Vault for secrets management
Enable Container Registry for Docker images
Enable Log Analytics Workspace (required for workspace-based App Insights and diagnostic logs). Recommended by MCSB.
Enable SQL Vulnerability Assessment (Express configuration). Recommended by MCSB.
Disable API-key (local) auth on Azure OpenAI and require managed identity. Set true for production to comply with MCSB. Only applies when deployAzureOpenAI = true.

Enable VNet integration: deploys a VNet, adds App Service outbound integration, and places SQL behind a private endpoint. Required to satisfy the MCAPS deny policy that blocks SQL servers with public network access enabled.
Name of the Azure OpenAI account
Location for the Azure OpenAI resource
Custom subdomain name for Azure OpenAI
Model name to deploy (e.g., gpt-4o, gpt-4, gpt-35-turbo)
Deployment name (defaults to model name if not specified)
Disable API-key (local) auth and require Entra ID / managed identity. Set true for production environments to comply with MCSB.
Name to use for the content-filter (RAI) policy applied to this account.
Resource ID of the Azure OpenAI account
Name of the Azure OpenAI account
Name of the RAI content-filter policy applied to this account
App Service outbound integration subnet prefix (min /28)
App Service integration subnet resource ID
Log Analytics workspace customer ID (used for queries)
Location for the Application Insights resource
Log Analytics workspace resource ID. When provided, ingestionMode is automatically set to LogAnalytics for workspace-based App Insights (recommended by MCSB).
Key Vault name (must be globally unique, 3-24 characters)
Enable Key Vault for template deployment
Enable Key Vault for disk encryption
Enable RBAC authorization (recommended over access policies)
Secrets to store in Key Vault
Secret URIs for app settings reference
Container Registry name (alphanumeric only, globally unique)
Enable admin user (not recommended for production)
Container Registry system-assigned managed identity principal ID
SQL Server name (must be globally unique)
Azure AD admin login name (UPN or display name, e.g. admin@contoso.com)

Azure AD admin Object ID - MUST be a valid GUID (xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx).
Get it with: az ad user show --id <UPN> --query id -o tsv
Leave empty to skip inline AD admin configuration.
WARNING: An invalid value (placeholder, email, empty path segment) causes ARM error
InvalidResourceIdSegment on parameters.properties.administrators.sid.
Database SKU (Basic, S0, S1, P1, etc.)
Enable SQL Vulnerability Assessment (Express configuration, no storage account needed). Recommended by MCSB (SecurityCenterBuiltIn).
Public network access for SQL server. Set Disabled for MCAPS compliance — connectivity must go through a private endpoint.
Restrict outbound network access for SQL server. Set Enabled for MCAPS compliance — prevents SQL from making arbitrary outbound connections outside allowed resources.

properties.administrators: [if(and(not(empty(parameters('azureAdAdminLogin'))), not(empty(parameters('azureAdAdminSid')))), createObject('administratorType', 'ActiveDirectory', 'login', parameters('azureAdAdminLogin'), 'sid', parameters('azureAdAdminSid'), 'tenantId', subscription().tenantId, 'azureADOnlyAuthentication', parameters('azureADOnlyAuthentication')), null())]

metadata.description: SQL Server system-assigned managed identity principal ID
Base name for private endpoint and related resources
Resource ID of the private endpoint subnet
Resource ID of the VNet for DNS zone link

appSettings.value: [concat(createArray(createObject('name', 'AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT', 'value', if(parameters('deployAzureOpenAI'), reference('azureOpenAIModule').outputs.endpoint.value, parameters('azureOpenAIEndpoint'))), createObject('name', 'AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT', 'value', if(parameters('deployAzureOpenAI'), reference('azureOpenAIModule').outputs.deploymentName.value, parameters('azureOpenAIDeployment'))), createObject('name', 'AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION', 'value', parameters('azureOpenAIApiVersion')), createObject('name', 'AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY', 'value', parameters('azureOpenAIApiKey')), createObject('name', 'PROJECT_ENDPOINT', 'value', parameters('projectEndpoint')), createObject('name', 'CONNECTION_NAME', 'value', parameters('connectionName')), createObject('name', 'MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME', 'value', parameters('modelDeploymentName')), createObject('name', 'FABRIC_WORKSPACE_ID', 'value', parameters('fabricWorkspaceId')), createObject('name', 'ORCHESTRATOR_AGENT_ID', 'value', parameters('orchestratorAgentId')), createObject('name', 'SALES_AGENT_ID', 'value', parameters('salesAgentId')), createObject('name', 'REALTIME_AGENT_ID', 'value', parameters('realtimeAgentId')), createObject('name', 'ANALYTICS_AGENT_ID', 'value', parameters('analyticsAgentId')), createObject('name', 'FINANCIAL_AGENT_ID', 'value', parameters('financialAgentId')), createObject('name', 'SUPPORT_AGENT_ID', 'value', parameters('supportAgentId')), createObject('name', 'OPERATIONS_AGENT_ID', 'value', parameters('operationsAgentId')), createObject('name', 'CUSTOMER_SUCCESS_AGENT_ID', 'value', parameters('customerSuccessAgentId')), createObject('name', 'OPERATIONS_EXCELLENCE_AGENT_ID', 'value', parameters('operationsExcellenceAgentId')), createObject('name', 'USE_FOUNDRY_AGENTS', 'value', 'true'), createObject('name', 'POWERBI_WORKSPACE_ID', 'value', parameters('powerbiWorkspaceId')), createObject('name', 'POWERBI_REPORT_ID', 'value', parameters('powerbiReportId')), createObject('name', 'POWERBI_CLIENT_ID', 'value', parameters('powerbiClientId')), createObject('name', 'POWERBI_TENANT_ID', 'value', parameters('powerbiTenantId')), createObject('name', 'POWERBI_CLIENT_SECRET', 'value', parameters('powerbiClientSecret')), createObject('name', 'SQL_SERVER', 'value', variables('sqlServerFqdn')), createObject('name', 'SQL_DATABASE', 'value', parameters('sqlDatabaseName')), createObject('name', 'SQL_USE_AZURE_AUTH', 'value', string(parameters('sqlUseAzureAuth'))), createObject('name', 'SQL_USERNAME', 'value', if(parameters('sqlUseAzureAuth'), '', parameters('sqlAdminUsername'))), createObject('name', 'SQL_PASSWORD', 'value', if(parameters('sqlUseAzureAuth'), '', parameters('sqlAdminPassword'))), createObject('name', 'ENABLE_AUTHENTICATION', 'value', string(parameters('enableAuthentication'))), createObject('name', 'JWT_SECRET_KEY', 'value', variables('finalJwtSecret')), createObject('name', 'JWT_SECRET', 'value', variables('finalJwtSecret')), createObject('name', 'JWT_ALGORITHM', 'value', parameters('jwtAlgorithm')), createObject('name', 'JWT_EXPIRATION_MINUTES', 'value', string(parameters('jwtExpirationMinutes'))), createObject('name', 'JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS', 'value', string(div(parameters('jwtExpirationMinutes'), 60))), createObject('name', 'APP_PORT', 'value', string(parameters('appPort'))), createObject('name', 'ENABLE_TRACING', 'value', string(parameters('enableTracing'))), createObject('name', 'LOG_LEVEL', 'value', parameters('logLevel')), createObject('name', 'SCM_DO_BUILD_DURING_DEPLOYMENT', 'value', 'true'), createObject('name', 'ENABLE_ORYX_BUILD', 'value', 'true'), createObject('name', 'WEBSITES_CONTAINER_START_TIME_LIMIT', 'value', '1800'), createObject('name', 'WEBSITE_HTTPLOGGING_RETENTION_DAYS', 'value', '7')), if(parameters('enableApplicationInsights'), createArray(createObject('name', 'APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING', 'value', reference('appInsightsModule').outputs.connectionString.value), createObject('name', 'ApplicationInsightsAgent_EXTENSION_VERSION', 'value', '~3')), createArray()))]

metadata.description: Log Analytics Workspace resource ID for diagnostic settings. Leave empty to skip.
VNet subnet resource ID for regional VNet integration (outbound). Leave empty to skip.
Web App system-assigned managed identity principal ID
Web App system-assigned managed identity tenant ID
Principal ID (managed identity) to assign the role to
Role definition ID (GUID of the built-in or custom role)
Target resource ID to assign the role on
Web App managed identity principal ID

nextSteps.value:
========================================
🎉 INFRASTRUCTURE DEPLOYMENT SUCCESSFUL!
========================================

📋 NEXT STEPS:

1️⃣  Grant SQL Database Access to Managed Identity
   ${sqlUseAzureAuth ? '   Run this in Azure Portal → SQL Query Editor:\n   \n   CREATE USER [${appServiceModule.outputs.webAppName}] FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER;\n   ALTER ROLE db_owner ADD MEMBER [${appServiceModule.outputs.webAppName}];' : '   Skipped - Using SQL authentication'}

2️⃣  Deploy Application Code
   
   cd azure-deployment-template
   ./scripts/deploy-code.ps1 -AppName ${appServiceModule.outputs.webAppName} -ResourceGroup ${resourceGroup().name}

3️⃣  Verify Deployment
   
   🌐 App URL: ${appServiceModule.outputs.webAppUrl}
   🔐 Default Login: admin / Admin@123
   
   ⚠️  IMPORTANT: Change default admin password immediately!

4️⃣  Monitor Application
   
   📊 Application Insights: ${enableApplicationInsights ? 'Enabled' : 'Disabled'}
   📝 Logs: az webapp log tail --name ${appServiceModule.outputs.webAppName} -g ${resourceGroup().name}

========================================
